meta.project: pgetl — Profuturo AFP market-data ETL & analytics platform
meta.purpose: Database architecture context for future sessions. Captures DDLs, keys, relationships, conventions, and open decisions.
meta.source_of_truth: Transcribed from the live PostgreSQL files under \\PFLMVIPR1FS0222\Area de Inversiones\5.Database\pgetl\src\db\schema\ (NOT schema_old\, which is the retired SQLite version).
meta.transcription_caveat: Settled tables (01-21) transcribed from screenshots and should be verified against the files. Tables 22-24 are UNDER CONSTRUCTION and shown in proposed-coherent form, not as currently on disk.

engine.migrated_from: SQLite (retired; old DDL preserved in src/db/schema_old/)
engine.connection: @contextmanager get_connection() -> yields conn, auto-commit on success, rollback+raise on error, close in finally. Callers use `with get_connection() as conn:` and must NOT call commit/rollback/close manually.
engine.encoding_note: Files are saved UTF-8 with BOM, Windows CRLF.

conventions.file_naming: NN_<name>.sql under src/db/schema/, two-digit zero-padded prefix, applied in numeric order.
conventions.header_comment: First line is the numbered filename, e.g. `-- 17_stg_prices_sbs_vector_completo.sql`.
conventions.surrogate_pk: INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY (PG identity, replaced SQLite AUTOINCREMENT).
conventions.numeric_facts: DOUBLE PRECISION for prices/analytics across fact_prices and all SBS/Bloomberg staging (replaced SQLite REAL). NOTE: positions domain currently uses NUMERIC — see open_decisions.
conventions.dates: DATE for business/reference dates (replaced SQLite TEXT 'YYYY-MM-DD').
conventions.timestamps: TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP for audit/load columns (replaced SQLite TEXT datetime('now')).
conventions.audit_columns: created_at + updated_at on dimensions; loaded_at on staging/fact load tables.
conventions.internal_code: procode TEXT NOT NULL is the house name for an internal code (dim_entity, dim_portfolio).
conventions.index_prefix: idx_<table>_<col(s)> is the house standard. The positions domain (22/23/24) currently deviates with ix_ — normalize to idx_.
conventions.booleans: BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE (replaced SQLite INTEGER 0/1 CHECK).
conventions.nullable_enum_checks: CHECK (col IN (...) OR col IS NULL) — explicit IS NULL rather than NULL inside the IN list.

lifecycle_status.delimiter_warning: series_registry mixes delimiters: 'backfill-pending' (hyphen) vs 'error_hold' (underscore). dim_portfolio's banner writes 'error-hold' (hyphen). Pick ONE literal set and reuse verbatim everywhere that inherits this lifecycle.

modeling_principles.entity_grain: An entity = a tradeable instrument/security, NOT an issuer. Each distinct security (equity class, each bond) is its own dim_entity row with its own ISIN.
modeling_principles.issuer_relationship: Issuer/parent links are represented separately (e.g. via dim_entity_identifiers / parent linkage), never by collapsing siblings into one entity_id.
modeling_principles.why: series_registry uniqueness is (entity_id, field, source); sharing an entity_id across instruments would collide (e.g. two PX_LAST series for the same issuer).
modeling_principles.x_isin: SBS X-ISIN instruments are registered as always-separate entities for the same reason.
modeling_principles.cash_positions: fact_positions expects cash to point security_entity_id at synthetic per-currency dim_entity rows with entity_type='cash' — BUT dim_entity's CHECK does not currently allow 'cash'. See known_inconsistencies.

dim_entity.purpose: Anchor table for all entities (securities, macro variables, etc.). One row per unique real-world entity regardless of domain.
dim_entity.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dim_entity (
    entity_id   INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
    procode     TEXT NOT NULL,
    entity_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (entity_type IN ('security','macro','index','fund')),
    name        TEXT,
    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

dim_security.purpose: Domain-specific attributes for entity_type='security'. SCD Type 1 for now (attributes overwritten in place).
dim_security.note: parsekyable and isin columns exist commented-out in the source; identifiers live in dim_entity_identifiers instead.
dim_security.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dim_security (
    security_id   INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
    entity_id     INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE REFERENCES dim_entity (entity_id),
    ticker        TEXT,
    name          TEXT,
    short_name    TEXT,
    security_name TEXT,
    sec_num_des   TEXT,
    currency      TEXT,
    country       TEXT,
    exchange      TEXT,
    market_sector TEXT,
    security_type TEXT,
    created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

dim_security_fund.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dim_security_fund (
    security_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES dim_security (security_id),
    fund_type   TEXT,
    asset_class TEXT,
    geography   TEXT,
    objective   TEXT,
    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

dim_security_bond.purpose: Bond-specific extension of dim_security. Referenced by SBS rf_local / rf_exterior staging comments ('also fed to dim_security_bond'), but its DDL was not visible in the source screenshots (file 05 body not shown). Fill in from the actual file before relying on it.

series_registry.purpose: Operational registry, one row per (entity, field, source). Spine of the system: pipelines query here at runtime to decide what to process and how.
series_registry.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS series_registry (
    series_id          INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
    entity_id          INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES dim_entity (entity_id),
    field              TEXT NOT NULL,
    domain             TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (domain IN ('prices','fundamentals','macro')),
    source             TEXT NOT NULL,
    frequency          TEXT NOT NULL,
    default_start_date DATE,
    status             TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'backfill-pending'
        CHECK (status IN ('backfill-pending','active','suspended','inactive','error_hold')),
    release_pattern    TEXT CHECK (release_pattern IN ('fixed','irregular','poll') OR release_pattern IS NULL),
    release_lag_days   INTEGER,
    allow_revisions    BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    revision_lookback  INTEGER,
    last_run_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
    last_run_status    TEXT CHECK (last_run_status IN ('success','failure','partial') OR last_run_status IS NULL),
    last_loaded_date   DATE,
    created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (entity_id, field, source)
);

fact_prices.purpose: Daily price observations. Pure long format, one value per row. No revisions expected — upsert on (series_id, date) with DO NOTHING.
fact_prices.note: Source banner has a typo: 'upsert on (series_id, reference_data)'. The real PK is (series_id, date).
fact_prices.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS fact_prices (
    series_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES series_registry (series_id),
    date      DATE NOT NULL,
    price     DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL,
    source    TEXT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (series_id, date)
);

dim_entity_identifiers.purpose: Maps internal entity_id to all vendor-specific identifiers. One row per (entity_id, id_type, source). Universal translator between internal surrogate keys and vendor-native identifiers used by pipeline extractors. Populated from seeds/identifiers.csv and enrichment pipelines.

indexes.name: idx_entity_identifiers_value
indexes.note: resolves entity_id from a vendor identifier

indexes.name: idx_entity_identifiers_entity
indexes.note: retrieves all identifiers for an entity

dim_entity_identifiers.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dim_entity_identifiers (
    entity_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES dim_entity (entity_id),
    id_type    TEXT NOT NULL,   -- parsekyable / isin / cusip / sedol / sbs
    id_value   TEXT NOT NULL,
    source     TEXT NOT NULL,
    is_primary BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (entity_id, id_type, source)
);

stg_security_bloomberg.purpose: Raw security attributes as returned by Bloomberg API. Long format: one row per (parsekyable, field, loaded_at). Populated by pipelines/dim/bloomberg/security/extract.py. Resolution step reads this and writes dim_security + dim_entity_identifiers. Rows never deleted (audit + re-resolution).
stg_security_bloomberg.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stg_security_bloomberg (
    stg_id      INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
    parsekyable TEXT NOT NULL,
    field       TEXT NOT NULL,
    value       TEXT,
    loaded_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (parsekyable, field, loaded_at)
);

stg_prices_bloomberg.purpose: Raw price observations as returned by Bloomberg API. Long format: one row per (parsekyable, field, date). Fact loader reads this and writes fact_prices. Retained after load for audit and re-processing.
stg_prices_bloomberg.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stg_prices_bloomberg (
    stg_id      INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
    parsekyable TEXT NOT NULL,
    field       TEXT NOT NULL,
    date        DATE NOT NULL,
    value       DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL,
    loaded_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (parsekyable, field, date, loaded_at)
);

stg_prices_sbs_vector_completo.purpose: Staging for SBS daily general prices file (vector_completo). Shared columns across all instrument types. One row per instrument per reference_date per load. Loaded by pipeline/prices/sbs/vector_completo/extract.py.
stg_prices_sbs_vector_completo.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stg_prices_sbs_vector_completo (
    codigo_sbs       TEXT NOT NULL,
    isin             TEXT,
    nemonico         TEXT,
    tipo_instrumento TEXT,
    emisor           TEXT,
    moneda           TEXT,
    precio           DOUBLE PRECISION,
    variacion        DOUBLE PRECISION,
    date             DATE NOT NULL,
    loaded_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (codigo_sbs, date, loaded_at)
);

stg_prices_sbs_rf_local.purpose: Staging for SBS local fixed income file (rf_local). Full bond analytics: YTM, spreads, duration, ratings, coupon dates, clean/dirty prices. One row per instrument per reference_date per load. Also feeds dim_security_bond.

columns.name: origen_precio
columns.note: source of price (e.g. BVL, Bloomberg, SBS)

stg_prices_sbs_rf_local.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stg_prices_sbs_rf_local (
    codigo_sbs              TEXT NOT NULL,
    isin                    TEXT,
    nemonico                TEXT,
    tipo_instrumento        TEXT,
    emisor                  TEXT,
    moneda                  TEXT,
    valor_facial            DOUBLE PRECISION,
    origen_precio           TEXT,
    fecha_emision           DATE,
    fecha_vencimiento       DATE,
    tasa_cupon              DOUBLE PRECISION,
    margen_libor            DOUBLE PRECISION,
    rating                  TEXT,
    ultimo_cupon            DATE,
    proximo_cupon           DATE,
    precio_limpio_monto     DOUBLE PRECISION,
    precio_limpio_pct       DOUBLE PRECISION,
    precio_sucio_monto      DOUBLE PRECISION,
    precio_sucio_pct        DOUBLE PRECISION,
    interes_corrido_monto   DOUBLE PRECISION,
    tir                     DOUBLE PRECISION,
    spreads                 DOUBLE PRECISION,
    tir_sin_opciones        DOUBLE PRECISION,
    duracion                DOUBLE PRECISION,
    variacion_precio_limpio DOUBLE PRECISION,
    variacion_precio_sucio  DOUBLE PRECISION,
    variacion_tir           DOUBLE PRECISION,
    date                    DATE NOT NULL,
    loaded_at               TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (codigo_sbs, date, loaded_at)
);

stg_prices_sbs_rf_exterior.purpose: Staging for SBS foreign fixed income file (rf_exterior). Similar to rf_local but fewer analytics fields — no TIR, spread, duration, or rating. Clean/dirty prices and coupon dates retained. Also feeds dim_security_bond.
stg_prices_sbs_rf_exterior.note: Unlike the other SBS staging tables, this one has NO nemonico column. Confirm whether that is intentional.
stg_prices_sbs_rf_exterior.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stg_prices_sbs_rf_exterior (
    codigo_sbs             TEXT NOT NULL,
    isin                   TEXT,
    tipo_instrumento       TEXT,
    emisor                 TEXT,
    moneda                 TEXT,
    valor_facial           DOUBLE PRECISION,
    origen_precio          TEXT,
    fecha_emision          DATE,
    fecha_vencimiento      DATE,
    tasa_cupon             DOUBLE PRECISION,
    ultimo_cupon           DATE,
    proximo_cupon          DATE,
    precio_limpio_monto    DOUBLE PRECISION,
    precio_limpio_pct      DOUBLE PRECISION,
    precio_sucio_monto     DOUBLE PRECISION,
    precio_sucio_pct       DOUBLE PRECISION,
    interes_corrido_monto  DOUBLE PRECISION,
    variacion_precio_sucio DOUBLE PRECISION,
    date                   DATE NOT NULL,
    loaded_at              TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (codigo_sbs, date, loaded_at)
);

stg_prices_sbs_tipo_cambio.purpose: Staging for SBS FX rates file (tipo_cambio). Reports bid/ask for currency pairs in both original currency and PEN terms, from multiple sources (SBS, Bloomberg, etc.). One row per currency pair per source per reference_date per load. date is derived from the file's internal 'fecha' column rather than acquisition date.
stg_prices_sbs_tipo_cambio.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stg_prices_sbs_tipo_cambio (
    moneda_nocional    TEXT NOT NULL,
    moneda_contraparte TEXT NOT NULL,
    fuente             TEXT NOT NULL,
    bid_original       DOUBLE PRECISION,
    ask_original       DOUBLE PRECISION,
    pen_bid            DOUBLE PRECISION,
    pen_ask            DOUBLE PRECISION,
    var_bid            DOUBLE PRECISION,
    var_ask            DOUBLE PRECISION,
    date               DATE NOT NULL,
    loaded_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (moneda_nocional, moneda_contraparte, fuente, date, loaded_at)
);

stg_prices_sbs_dividendos.purpose: Staging for SBS daily dividends file (dividendos). Dividend adjustment factors and delivery types. One row per instrument per reference_date per load.
stg_prices_sbs_dividendos.note: Carries both fecha_vector (from file) and date (pipeline reference date).
stg_prices_sbs_dividendos.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stg_prices_sbs_dividendos (
    fecha_vector  DATE,
    codigo_sbs    TEXT NOT NULL,
    isin          TEXT,
    nemonico      TEXT,
    emisor        TEXT,
    moneda        TEXT,
    factor_ajuste DOUBLE PRECISION,
    tipo_entrega  TEXT,
    date          DATE NOT NULL,
    loaded_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (codigo_sbs, date, loaded_at)
);

dim_portfolio.purpose: One row per (procode, source). A 'portfolio' is anything that holds positions: own_account (FMS-sourced accounts we manage), regulator_filing (SBS-sourced peer filings), etf (Bloomberg or scraped holdings). The same logical portfolio from two sources is two rows; parent_entity_id links ETF portfolios back to their dim_entity row so price+holdings can be joined.
differs_from_current_file: Current file column is procode but banner comment still says '(internal_code, source)' — stale comment.
Current file uses ix_ index prefix; normalized here to idx_.
status CHECK constraint added here (current file declares status without the lifecycle CHECK).
dim_portfolio.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dim_portfolio (
    portfolio_id     INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
    procode          TEXT NOT NULL,
    source           TEXT NOT NULL,            -- fms | sbs | bloomberg | scraper
    portfolio_type   TEXT NOT NULL,            -- own_account | regulator_filing | etf
    display_name     TEXT,
    base_currency    TEXT,
    parent_entity_id INTEGER,                  -- links ETFs back to dim_entity
    status           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'
        CHECK (status IN ('backfill-pending','active','suspended','inactive','error_hold')),
    created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (procode, source),
    FOREIGN KEY (parent_entity_id) REFERENCES dim_entity (entity_id)
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dim_portfolio_source ON dim_portfolio (source);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dim_portfolio_status ON dim_portfolio (status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dim_portfolio_parent ON dim_portfolio (parent_entity_id);

fact_positions.purpose: Daily holdings, multi-source ready. One row per holding per portfolio per day per source; source on the PK so the same logical holding from two sources (e.g. an own account that also appears in an SBS filing) doesn't collide. Cash positions point security_entity_id at synthetic per-currency dim_entity rows (entity_type='cash'), keeping this table uniform (no separate fact_cash_positions). weight is 0..1, MV-based, computed at transform time per (portfolio_id, date). Upsert is ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE since positions get restated.

columns.name: date
columns.note: PENDING: current file uses as_of_date; see pending_decisions

differs_from_current_file: Current file uses as_of_date; proposed here as date for coherence with fact_prices + all staging. UNRESOLVED — see pending_decisions.
Current file uses NUMERIC for numeric facts; proposed DOUBLE PRECISION to match the rest of the schema. UNRESOLVED.
Current file uses ix_ index prefix; normalized to idx_.

Keep security_entity_id (NOT bare entity_id): this is a portfolio x security x date intersection with two FKs, so the role qualifier disambiguates from portfolio_id (cf. parent_entity_id). This reverses the June-10 'match fact_prices / NOT as_of_date / NOT security_id' note.
fact_positions.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS fact_positions (
    portfolio_id       INTEGER NOT NULL,
    security_entity_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    date               DATE NOT NULL,
    source             TEXT NOT NULL,          -- fms | sbs | bloomberg | scraper
    quantity           DOUBLE PRECISION,
    market_value       DOUBLE PRECISION,
    cost_basis         DOUBLE PRECISION,
    accrued_interest   DOUBLE PRECISION,
    weight             DOUBLE PRECISION,       -- 0..1, portfolio weight by MV
    price_used         DOUBLE PRECISION,
    currency           TEXT,
    yield_to_maturity  DOUBLE PRECISION,
    duration           DOUBLE PRECISION,
    loaded_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (portfolio_id, security_entity_id, date, source),
    FOREIGN KEY (portfolio_id)       REFERENCES dim_portfolio (portfolio_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (security_entity_id) REFERENCES dim_entity (entity_id)
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_fact_positions_date    ON fact_positions (date);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_fact_positions_pf_date ON fact_positions (portfolio_id, date);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_fact_positions_sec     ON fact_positions (security_entity_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_fact_positions_source  ON fact_positions (source);

stg_positions_fms.purpose: Raw landing for FMS sproc output. Permissive types, no FKs. Each run tags its rows with batch_id (e.g. 'fms_20260514_081532') so transform can scope to its own batch and replay/audit historical loads. raw_payload holds the full sproc row as JSON; if the sproc adds a column not yet mapped in extract.py it survives here and can be backfilled without re-hitting FMS.

columns.name: loaded_at
columns.note: PENDING: current file calls this extracted_at

columns.name: raw_payload
columns.note: JSON of the full sproc row, for audit. PENDING: current file uses TEXT

differs_from_current_file: Current file calls the load timestamp extracted_at; proposed loaded_at for staging coherence (batch_id already carries the extraction time).
Current file uses NUMERIC; proposed DOUBLE PRECISION.
Current file uses raw_payload TEXT; proposed JSONB (more idiomatic/queryable in PG).
Current file uses ix_ prefix; normalized to idx_.
FMS id_type for instrument codes is 'fms_instrument_id' (registered in dim_entity_identifiers).
stg_positions_fms.ddl:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stg_positions_fms (
    batch_id          TEXT NOT NULL,           -- e.g. 'fms_20260514_081532'
    loaded_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    date              DATE,
    account_code      TEXT,                    -- FMS internal account id
    instrument_id     TEXT,                    -- FMS internal instrument id
    isin              TEXT,
    ticker            TEXT,
    description       TEXT,
    quantity          DOUBLE PRECISION,
    market_value      DOUBLE PRECISION,
    cost_basis        DOUBLE PRECISION,
    accrued_interest  DOUBLE PRECISION,
    currency          TEXT,
    price_used        DOUBLE PRECISION,
    yield_to_maturity DOUBLE PRECISION,
    duration          DOUBLE PRECISION,
    raw_payload       JSONB                    -- full sproc row, for audit
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stg_positions_fms_batch ON stg_positions_fms (batch_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stg_positions_fms_date  ON stg_positions_fms (date);

relationships.note: UNIQUE FK; a security is one entity
nullable; links ETF portfolios to their entity
All stg_* (Bloomberg, SBS, FMS) tables are FK-free permissive landing zones; resolution/transform steps join them to dims by vendor identifier via dim_entity_identifiers.

pipeline_flow.prices: vendor extract.py -> stg_prices_* -> fact loader resolves entity via dim_entity_identifiers + series via series_registry -> fact_prices (upsert DO NOTHING).
pipeline_flow.securities: bloomberg security extract.py -> stg_security_bloomberg -> resolution -> dim_security + dim_entity_identifiers.
pipeline_flow.positions: FMS sproc -> stg_positions_fms (batch-tagged) -> transform resolves entity + portfolio -> fact_positions (upsert DO UPDATE). SBS filings and Bloomberg/scraped ETF holdings are the other two position sources.

known_inconsistencies.issue: 'error_hold' (underscore) in series_registry CHECK vs 'error-hold' (hyphen) in dim_portfolio banner; 'backfill-pending' uses a hyphen. Standardize the literal set and reuse verbatim.

fact_positions expects synthetic cash entities with entity_type='cash', but dim_entity CHECK only allows ('security','macro','index','fund'). Add 'cash' to the CHECK or change the cash-handling approach.
Says 'upsert on (series_id, reference_data)'; real PK is (series_id, date).

Says 'one row per (internal_code, source)' but column/constraint is procode. (schema_old had the real bug UNIQUE(internal_code,...) against a procode column; the PG version fixed the constraint, comment lagged.)
Missing nemonico, present in all sibling SBS staging tables. Confirm intent.
Uses ix_ while the rest of the schema uses idx_. Normalize to idx_.
Uses NUMERIC while fact_prices and SBS monetary fields use DOUBLE PRECISION. Decide one policy schema-wide.

open_decisions.question: fact_positions date column: 'date' (coherence with fact_prices + staging, 1:1 stg->fact map) vs 'as_of_date' (self-documenting snapshot semantics).
NUMERIC vs DOUBLE PRECISION for monetary/position facts across positions domain.
open_decisions.default_recommendation: DOUBLE PRECISION to match existing schema; switch to NUMERIC only if cent-exact reconciliation is required (and then revisit SBS _monto fields too).

open_decisions.question: Add composite PK (batch_id, account_code, instrument_id, date) to stg_positions_fms for within-batch dedup, mirroring SBS staging PKs?
open_decisions.default_recommendation: Optional; leave PK-less if intra-batch duplicates are acceptable for the permissive landing zone.

open_decisions.question: Add CHECK on dim_portfolio.portfolio_type (closed set own_account|regulator_filing|etf)?
open_decisions.default_recommendation: Optional; source stays free-TEXT to match the rest of the schema.

not_captured.schema_file_number_gaps: Source screenshots did not show files 05, 06, 07, 08, 11, 12, 15 in schema/. Confirm what occupies them.
referenced_but_unseen_tables: dim_security_bond (file 05, referenced by SBS rf staging)
likely fact_fundamentals / fact_macro (series_registry.domain allows 'fundamentals' and 'macro', implying parallel fact tables not shown)
not_captured.action: Capture these from the live files and append to this context before relying on it as complete.
